75449.subject: рҹҹӘ The other American Revolution
75449.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

вҖңSince 1971, when the United States indefinitely suspended its promise to convert paper dollars into gold, we all use Massachusetts currency.вҖқ
_вҖ” __Dror Goldberg__ _



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/33252039-f3d3-48a3-93b7-49115c79b214/image.png?t=1783008244)
Caption: 

# The other American Revolution: Fiat currency

The American colonies won their independence from Great Britain with a weapon of their own invention: fiat currency.

In 1690, the Massachusetts Bay Colony had made monetary history when it issued 7,000 GBP in вҖңbills of creditвҖқ to soldiers returning from a failed raid into Quebec.

The disgruntled soldiers had expected to be paid in plunder, and when that failed to materialize, the colony gave them newly printed paper money instead вҖ” paper that was valuable only because it was required for paying taxes.

It wasnвҖҷt the first paper_ _currency ever вҖ” that honor belongs to the Song Dynasty, which issued paper money in China roughly 700 years earlier. But those ancient notes were initially redeemable for copper coins.

The Massachusetts bills of credit, by contrast, were the first paper money in history to hold value _solely _because the issuing government required them for tax payments.

It was a neat trick: Print paper. Pay people with it. Require it for taxes. People then had to earn the paperвҖ”by soldiering, for exampleвҖ”to pay their taxes.

In other words, the colony printed itself an army.

The Massachusetts money was вҖңan invention on a global scale and a great intellectual breakthrough,вҖқ as Dror Goldberg documents in [Easy Money: American Puritans and the Invention of Modern Currency](https://www.amazon.com/Easy-Money-American-Invention-Governments-ebook/dp/B0BXX5G7TD/ref=sr_1_1?crid=1A06GRPHIJFIA&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.VxXZIFgtI1-2iniBdN3vx-Os7TFH24Sva69R6SZY-H0.qEJQBDSq5jp46XsnxeYybK2pTq2Qlg2_eNN2sLOD5u4&dib_tag=se&keywords=easy+money+dror+goldberg&qid=1751502757&sprefix=easy+money+dror+goldberg%2Caps%2C141&sr=8-1)_._

вҖңIt was a fundamental change in the legal foundation of money, shifting the anchor of money for the first time in history from the intrinsically valuable goods it was made of to the circulation of money into and out of the stateвҖҷs treasury.вҖқ

вҖң

75444.body:
Onchain Assets Weekly

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Securitize


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Securitize

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/onchain-assets-weekly-secz-special-edition-securitize-ay30c?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BlFMr9QzOQ5%2BiNXdn%2Br0hEQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=13GRDjKyRetYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mr58bfoz~p3-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mr58bfoz-p3
  
In a special edition of Onchain Assets Weekly: Securitize goes public. Now trading on the New York Stock Exchange under SECZ, this milestone marks a new chapter for Securitize вҖ” and an important moment for the future of tokenized securities. As the first newly public company to tokenize its own stock from day one, Securitize is bringing its mission to the public markets. HereвҖҷs what leading outlets are saying. Under the Radar: Securitize Stock Jumps in NYSE Debut | The Wall Street Journal The WSJ shares that Securitize shares traded as much as 16% higher in the tokenization firmвҖҷs NYSE debut. With Carlos adding, вҖңI think this is a good signal that crypto is growing up and it's just moving from more speculative assets into realвҖ‘world assets.вҖқ READ MORE Securitize Tokenizes $295 Million of its Own Stock on Solana and Avalanche Amid NYSE Debut | CoinDesk The newly-public company's shares are the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch, aiming to make a point against rival third-party stock token issuers. READ MORE BlackRock Partner Securitize Rides Wall Street Tokenization Wave | Bloomberg Bloomberg profiles Securitize's NYSE debut as a milestone for both the company and the broader tokenization market, validating the companyвҖҷs strategy as tokenized real-world assets move further into the financial mainstream. READ MORE вҖңThis Will Put the Word Tokenization and What it Means for Capital Markets Out ThereвҖқ | CNBCвҖҷs Fortt Knox Carlos talks to CNBCвҖҷs Jon Fortt about being the first pure-play tokenization firm to list on a major U.S. exchange, what this means for tokenization on the whole, and what the future holds. WATCH NOW вҖңTokenization Is the Most-Discussed Topic in the IndustryвҖқ | Schwab Network Carlos shares вҖңwhy nowвҖқ when it comes to Securitize going public and how, when it comes to tokenization, we are at the end of the beginning. вҖңThereвҖҷs a lot of growth ahead of us.вҖқ WATCH NOW Direct Ownership, From Day One | Taking Stock Deputy COO Suzy Singh talks to Taking StockвҖҷs J.D. Durkin about how SecuritizeвҖҷs common stock is already issued onchain and gives retail investors the ability to hold it directly in their own wallets. вҖңWeвҖҷre tokenizing the world.вҖқ WATCH NOW

75441.subject: рҹ”· The Daily: Bitcoin ETFs end $2.7 billion outflow streak
75441.body:
Spotify asks Kalshi, Polymarket to remove branding after manipulated streams used to settle music bets | Samsung, Dunamu say they were listed as OUSD stablecoin consortium members without official consultation | Bank of Russia governor says 'everything is ready' for widespread use of digital ruble ahead of September rollout | IMF says policy choices will determine whether tokenization strengthens or fragments the financial system |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Naga Avan-Nomayo

Presented by

BitGo ( https://www.bitgo.com/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Friday рҹҺү *Bitcoin* held above $61,000 ahead of the U.S. *Independence Day* holiday as softer-than-expected jobs data revived risk appetite, though analysts caution ( https://www.theblock.co/post/407131/markets-find-their-footing-bitcoin-holds-61000-rebound-ahead-of-us-independence-day-as-soft-jobs-data-eases-rate-fears?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) the *Fed* outlook remains far from settled.

In today's newsletter, *Bitcoin ETFs* break a 10-day outflow streak, *Spotify* asks *Kalshi* and *Polymarket* to strip its logo from their platforms, *South Korean* firms say they were listed as members of the *OUSD* consortium without consultation, and more.

Meanwhile, *Irish authorities* recover ( https://www.theblock.co/post/407062/irish-authorities-recover-another-500-bitcoins-lost-clifton-collins-drug-stash?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) another 500 BTC from the lost *Clifton Collins* -linked drug stash.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вҖ” and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рҹ’ё US bitcoin ETFs break 10-day negative streak with $222 million worth of inflows*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/407110/us-bitcoin-etfs-222-million-inflows?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

U.S. spot *bitcoin* ETFs logged $221.7 million in net inflows on July 2, breaking a 10-day outflow streak, according to SoSoValue data.

* *Fidelity's FBTC* led with $166 million in inflows, followed by *Ark Invest and 21Shares' ARKB* with $91.8 million and *VanEck's HODL* with $4.4 million.
* The inflows mark the first positive day since June 16, after a losing streak that totaled more than $2.7 billion.
* *BlackRock's IBIT* was the only fund to see net outflows on Thursday, shedding $40.4 million in its 11th

75440.subject: Happy birthday America, the ally Europe admiresвҖ”uneasily
75440.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjZ9AAcAAAAAA4bir4sJmNvrifr3nCgfbbaJ-Emclh_a6sUlVdh4S-5v26ffheyBRPyUZfSd88Tx0zrO8KW_Sy8XrYeOyvXA3af5dNlQdSb9CtT8mswwgCjDqg 

July 3rd 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjZ9AAcAAAAAA4bir4sKwMCYU-3A1lOA5Y_5fQ-u3HMeNyvNd2ND7gM1Uv_S9gr9874BEC4SfIQPffIaY3T-otIa6XAPMAaOK37vuQJFT86tTpKw4EIUJGWhvg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjZ9AAcAAAAAA4bir4sLIFERoHg1Vrm57W8GC-iTuioW7Xil7f1w5JhtzUyfSvuMOL9dCnVPOekJl42FVKkdr5I1IzxKUACaBPefqW3sXzIr7dltJXJklN-UXQ 


 


 
 

Lane Greene

Senior digital editor



America is undeniably richer and more powerful than Europe. Some Europeans sneer that comes with costs (in quality of life, inequality or environmental damage) which they simply wouldn’t put up with. Maybe. But as the new kid turns 250, the two sides can learn from each other without having to 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjZ9AAcAAAAAA4bir4sM5kSGJZAudysJiwMBnSfYn1Yd1x910Pg-lgKeJ6GUtzs5U630W8h7i1eR501aUylTL-UJTrvfD5nnHBvyX4p0Q_xeiq8u4r5b 
import each other’s flaws .


We said oil prices were in “la-la land” and had to go much higher as a result of the Iran war. 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjZ9AAcAAAAAA4bir4sNSmc69kHIS1kT16B0zeQnnmF_a4M08JAw-h2lEaltMkx7rL77pR
